channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel seizes strategic hilltop in Lebanon as U.S.-Iran talks remain in limbo
item.description: Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon capturing a strategic hilltop as U.S. talks aimed at ending the war with Iran appear to be in limbo.
item.content:encoded: Israel expands its invasion of Lebanon capturing a strategic hilltop as U.S. talks aimed at ending the war with Iran appear to be in limbo.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: State Department official on the potential for a deal to end the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott about the White House's confidence in closing a peace deal with Iran
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott about the White House's confidence in closing a peace deal with Iran
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Is bovine colostrum really 'liquid gold' for gut health?
item.description: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.
item.content:encoded: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel expands Lebanon offensive as U.S.-Iran peace talks stall, Congress returns to D.C. with long to-do list, rulings create more obstacles for Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund.
item.content:encoded: Israel expands Lebanon offensive as U.S.-Iran peace talks stall, Congress returns to D.C. with long to-do list, rulings create more obstacles for Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Funds for Trump's Board of Peace aren't in World Bank account. So where are they?
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Financial Times reporter Abigail Hauslohner about the funding of President Trump's Board of Peace to oversee Gaza's reconstruction.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Financial Times reporter Abigail Hauslohner about the funding of President Trump's Board of Peace to oversee Gaza's reconstruction.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: After Russian missile strike, owners of Ukrainian coffee shop pledge to rebuild
item.description: After a large-scale Russian missile strike hit downtown Kyiv, the owners of a coffee shop that opened just hours earlier were already serving coffee again and planning to rebuild.
item.content:encoded: After a large-scale Russian missile strike hit downtown Kyiv, the owners of a coffee shop that opened just hours earlier were already serving coffee again and planning to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Palamarenko

item.title: Likely Democratic nominee Graham Platner talks about Maine's upcoming Senate race
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks likely Democratic nominee Graham Platner how he plans to beat incumbent Republican Susan Collins in Maine's Senate race.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks likely Democratic nominee Graham Platner how he plans to beat incumbent Republican Susan Collins in Maine's Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: After musicians drop out of Freedom 250, Trump floats rally featuring him
item.description: Over the weekend, President Trump suggested an event celebrating America's 250th birthday should instead be a rally with him as the headliner after many of the artists slated to perform dropped out.
item.content:encoded: Over the weekend, President Trump suggested an event celebrating America's 250th birthday should instead be a rally with him as the headliner after many of the artists slated to perform dropped out.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Iranians in L.A. await team's World Cup games with complicated emotions
item.description: Los Angeles is home to a huge Iranian diaspora and is slated to host World Cup matches where Iran will play. How is that diaspora feeling about the coming World Cup amidst the U.S. war on Iran?
item.content:encoded: Los Angeles is home to a huge Iranian diaspora and is slated to host World Cup matches where Iran will play. How is that diaspora feeling about the coming World Cup amidst the U.S. war on Iran?
item.dc:creator: Libby Rainey

item.title: After Memorial Day break, Congress returns to D.C. with long to-do list
item.description: Congress returns to Washington with a long to-do list, including disentangling immigration enforcement funding from the President's weaponization fund.
item.content:encoded: Congress returns to Washington with a long to-do list, including disentangling immigration enforcement funding from the President's weaponization fund.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel
